title: What does the woman with money wear on a Tuesday?
subtitle: A journal prompt set for mapping the ordinary-day details of the identity you're stepping into.
excerpt: A journal prompt set from Day 22 of the MONEY program. Use to move beyond the abstract idea of a wealthy identity and into the concrete details of how it looks, feels, and behaves on an unremarkable weekday.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 22 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and the Week 4 daily prompts of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798276631448).

practice.whenToUse: Use when the wealthy identity feels abstract or far away, and you want to map its details into something concrete and daily.

content.text: A journal prompt set from Day 22 of the MONEY program. The prompts ask for specifics: not the lifestyle of the wealthy version of you at her best, but what she does and chooses on an ordinary Tuesday. Twenty minutes, no editing.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
How would I think, act, and make choices if I already knew money was always available to me?
Write quickly, no filter. What would be different about today specifically?
What in my life today already reflects stability and abundance?
Find the evidence that's already there, however small.
What does the woman with money wear on a Tuesday?
Not the big occasion. The ordinary day. Write the specific details.
What does she eat for breakfast? How does she start her morning?
The small everyday decisions. What are they?
What is one choice I could make today that she would make?
One concrete, available action. Write it as a decision, not a wish.
Adapted from Day 22 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and the Week 4 daily prompts of The Money Journal (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
